_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma or equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Child has a squint all the time
Child is > 3 months of age and has a squint that comes and goes
Child regularly turns its head to one side or keeping one eye closed when looking at things
Squint or double vision later in life
Recommended if other treatments are not suitable or do not help

clinical_thresholds.note: for squints that come and go

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing which muscle is affected

claim.label: Intraoperative photograph with patient ID, date and time (optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes including history, clinical symptoms (diplopia, etc.) and ocular examination (misalignment of the eyes, etc.) suggestive of Squint?
Clinical photo of Affected part with Proper labelling of Affected Eye whether R or L with full face photograph?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed, and outcomes of the successful?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Was the intra operative photograph submitted?

common_queries: Evidence of prior conservative management for squint is not documented or does not indicate failure to treat, requiring surgery.
Clinical notes do not detail the affected muscle.
Clinical photograph is missing, not of affected part, lacks R/L labelling, or is not a full face photograph.
Operative notes are missing or do not detail surgical steps and outcomes.
Documents for post-operative care or follow-up advice are not available or insufficient.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Squint correction surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
surgical_options: Surgery- in cases when glasses are not completely effective
contraindications: Babies <= 3 months with squints that come and go (are not usually a cause for concern)
